title: ranunculus ophioglossifolius var. fontanus
intro: Ranunculus ophioglossifolius var. fontanus, also known as the Mountain Buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its small white flowers and shiny leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, including protoanemonin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus ophioglossifolius var. fontanus include its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial infections. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically used in tincture or salve form, applied topically to the affected area to promote healing and prevent infection.
